steps.title: Combine x terms
steps.content: Add $x$ to both sides to gather all $x$-terms on the left: $$13x+x=42 \implies 14x=42.$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $14$: $$x=\tfrac{42}{14}=3.$$ The answer is 3.

This works because adding $x$ to both sides preserves the equation while combining the two variable terms into one coefficient.
